post.description:
Imports data using a cloud connector. This creates an asynchronous import job in Tenable Vulnerability Management. You can check the import status by examining the `status_message` property in [connector details](ref:io-connectors-details).
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the connector for which to import the data.
parameters.name: connector_id

200.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully scheduled the cloud connector for import.

type.description: The type of cloud connector. Types include `aws`, `aws_keyless`, `aws_fa`, `azure`, `azure_fa`, `gcp`, or `gcp_keyless`.

data_type.description: The data type imported by the connector. This value is always `assets`.

name.description: The name of the cloud connector. The name must be unique within a Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

status.description:
The import status of the connector. Status values can include:
 - Completed—Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully used the connector to import assets (no imports scheduled)
 - Scheduled—Imports using the connector are scheduled for future dates
 - Saved—Tenable Vulnerability Management saved the connector configuration, but did not import assets at this time (no imports scheduled)
 - Error—Tenable Vulnerability Management failed to import assets using the connector

is_fa.description: For AWS keyless connectors, indicates whether Frictionless Assessment is enabled or not.

status_message.description: Extended description of the connector status. If Frictionless Assessment is enabled, this property is set to `FA Enabled`. For information about connector error codes, see [Connectors](doc:io-connectors).

units.description: The units of time for the import interval. Units can include `days`, `hours`, `minutes`.

value.description: The number of units between import intervals.

date_created.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the connector was created, for example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

date_modified.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the connector was last modified or new records were imported, for example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

id.description: The UUID of the connector.

container_uuid.description: The UUID of the Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

expired.description: Indicates whether the Vulnerability Management license for the Tenable Vulnerability Management instance associated with the connector is expired.

incremental_mode.description: Indicates whether a connector has completed the initial full import successfully. If the value is `true`, then the connector is in incremental mode where it imports assets based on on the service provider event stream instead of enumerating all assets in the account every single time.

params.description: The connector parameters. Note that only the `params` object for AWS keyed connectors is shown below. The `param` object for other connector types is not used in the example.

access_key.description: For AWS connectors, the AWS access key. Note: The `access_key` is not included in the keyless AWS connector parameters.

sub_accounts.description: For AWS connectors, a list of AWS accounts associated with the connector.

role_arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role generated based on the associated account ID.

external_id.description: The UUID of your Tenable Vulnerability Management instance used by AWS to identify it as a client application. You can obtain the UUID of your Tenable Vulnerability Management account using the [GET /users/{user_id}](ref:users-details) endpoint. The UUID corresponds to the `container_uuid` attribute of the response message for that endpoint.

trails.description: A list of AWS cloudtrails associated with the account.

arn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the cloudtrail.

name.description: The name of the cloudtrail.
The AWS region code, for example, `us-east-1`. The value of `All` indicates that the cloudtrail is associated with all AWS available regions.

friendly_name.description: The AWS region name, for example, `US East (N. Virginia)`. The value of `All` indicates that the cloudtrail is associated with all AWS available regions.

availability.description:
Indicates whether a cloudtrail is available to be used by a connector (logging is turned on in AWS, or it has at least one EventSelector with IncludeManagementEvents). Values include:
 - `success`—The cloudtrail is available.
 - `error`—The cloudtrail is not available.

incremental_mode.description: Indicates whether a connector has completed the initial full import successfully. If the value is `true`, then the connector is in incremental mode where it imports assets based on events instead of enumerating all assets in the account every single time.

status.description: For Azure connectors, a list of import status records.

last_event_seen.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the last time the connector found new or changed records and successfully imported them.

release_timestamp.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the last time the connector successfully completed an import (regardless of whether it found any changes).

message.description: The extended import status message.

targets.description: For Azure Frictionless Assessment connectors, the resource group and tag information.

**Note:** You can use the wildcard `*` to select all resource groups in the Azure subscription.

**Note:** You can use the wildcard `*` to select all tag names in Azure for Frictionless Assessment.

tag_value.description:

**Note:** You can use the wildcard `*` to select all tag values in Azure for Frictionless Assessment.

tenant_id.description: For Azure connectors, the Azure tenant ID (directory ID).

subscription_id.description: For Azure connectors, the Azure subscription ID. If you do not provide subscription IDs, Tenable Vulnerability Management automatically discovers them.

auto_discovery.description: Specifies whether Auto Discovery is enabled or not for AWS keyless connectors. If Auto Discovery is enabled, the connector will automatically discover linked accounts and CloudTrails.

service.description: The service targeted by the connector. Values include `aws`, `aws_keyless`, `aws_fa`, `azure`, `azure_fa`, and `gcp`.

last_sync_time.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time of the last successful import, for example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

network_uuid.description: The UUID of the [network](doc:manage-networks-tio) associated with the connector.

post.description:
Creates a managed credential object that you can use when configuring and running scans. You can grant other users the permission to use the managed credential object in scans and to edit the managed credential configuration.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

name.description: The name of the managed credential. This name must be unique within your Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

description.description: The description of the managed credential object.

type.description: The type of credential object. For a list of supported credential types, use the GET /credentials/types endpoint.

settings.description:
The configuration settings for the credential. The parameters of this object vary depending on the credential type. For more information, see [Determine Settings for a Credential Type](doc:determine-settings-for-credential-type).

**Note:** This form displays limited parameters that support a Windows type of credential that uses password authentication.

domain.description: The Windows domain to which the username belongs.

username.description: The username on the target system.

auth_method.description: The name for the authentication method. This value corresponds to the credentials[].types[].configuration[].options[].id attribute in the response message for the [GET /credentials/types](ref:credentials-list-credential-types) endpoint.

password.description: The user password on the target system.

permissions.description: A list of user permissions for the managed credential. If a request message omits this parameter, Tenable Vulnerability Management automatically creates a `permissions` object for the user account that submits the request.

grantee_uuid.description:
The UUID of the user or user group granted permissions for the managed credential.   

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

type.description:
A value specifying whether the grantee is a user (`user`) or a user group (`group`).  

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

permissions.description:
A value specifying the permissions granted to the user or user group for the credential. Possible values are:
 - 32—The user can view credential information and use the credential in scans. Corresponds to the **Can Use** permission in the user interface.
 - 64—The user can view and edit credential settings, delete the credential, and use the credential in scans. Corresponds to the **Can Edit** permission in the user interface.  

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

name.description:
The name of the user or user group that you want to grant permissions for the managed credential.  

This parameter is optional when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

example.name: Windows devices (Headquarters)
example.description: Use for scans of Windows devices located at headquarters.

200.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully creates a managed credential object.

uuid.description: The UUID of the new managed credential object.

403.description: Returned if you do not have permission to create managed credential objects.

409.description: Returned if a managed credential object with the same name already exists.

415.description: Returned if the request payload is in an unsupported format.

get.description:
Lists managed credentials where you have been assigned at least CAN USE (32) permissions. **Note:** This endpoint does not list scan-specific or policy-specific credentials (that is, credentials stored in either a scan or a policy). To view a list of scan-specific or policy-specific credentials, use the editor details endpoint (GET /editor/{type}/{id}).
Requires Can Use [32] credential permissions. See [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.description:
A filter condition in the following format: `field:operator:value`. For managed credentials, you can only filter on the `name` field, using the following operators: 
* eq—The name of the returned credential is equal to the text you specify. 
* neq—The returned list of managed credentials excludes the credential object where the name is equal to the text you specify. 
* match—The returned list includes managed credentials where the name contains the text you specify at least partially.

You can specify multiple `f` parameters, separated by ampersand (&) characters. If you specify multiple `f` parameters, use the `ft` parameter to specify how Tenable Vulnerability Management applies the multiple filter conditions.
parameters.name: f

parameters.description: The operator that Tenable Vulnerability Management applies if multiple \`f\` parameters are present. The `OR` and `AND` operators are supported. If you omit this parameter and multiple `f` parameters are present, Tenable Vulnerability Management applies the `AND` operator by default.
parameters.name: ft

parameters.description: The UUID of a scan owner. This parameter limits the returned data to managed credentials assigned to scans owned by the specified user.
parameters.name: referrer_owner_uuid

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `50`. This parameter has a minimum value of `1` and a maximum value of `300`.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `name:asc` would sort results by the `name` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

200.description: Returns a list of managed credentials.

uuid.description: The UUID of the managed credential object.

name.description: The name of the managed credential object. You specify the name when you create or update the managed credential.

description.description: The definition of the managed credential object. You can specify the description when you create or update the managed credential.

id.description: The system name that uniquely identifies the credential category.

name.description: The display name for the credential category in the user interface.

id.description: The system name that uniquely identifies the credential type.

name.description: The display name for the credential type in the user interface.

created_date.description: The date (in Unix time) when the managed credential object was created.

id.description: The ID of the user who created the credential.

display_name.description: The name of the user who created the credential.

id.description: The ID of the user who last used the credential in a scan.

display_name.description: The name of the user who last used the credential in a scan.

permissions.description: A value specifying the permissions granted to the user or user group for the credential. For possible values, see "Credential Roles" in [Permissions](doc:permissions).

user_permissions.description: The permissions for the managed credential that are assigned to the user account submitting the API request. For possible values, see "Credential Roles" in [Permissions](doc:permissions).

pagination.description: An object containing pagination information for the current result set.

total.description: The total number of records matching your search criteria.

limit.description: The number of records requested (or the default value if omitted from the request).

offset.description: The starting record you requested (or zero if omitted).

sort.description: An array of objects specifying how the current result set is sorted.

items.description: An object specifying how the current result set is sorted, including the property by which the results were sorted and the sort direction.

name.description: The property used to sort the results.

order.description: The sort direction for the results. `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending.

user_permissions.description: The permissions for the managed credential that are assigned to the user account submitting the API request. For possible values, see "Credential Roles" in [Permissions](doc:permissions).

settings.description: The configuration settings for the credential. The parameters of this object vary depending on the credential type. For more information, see [Determine Settings for a Credential Type](doc:determine-settings-for-credential-type).

grantee_uuid.description:
The UUID of the user or user group granted permissions for the managed credential.   

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

type.description:
A value specifying whether the grantee is a user (`user`) or a user group (`group`).  

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

permissions.description:
A value specifying the permissions granted to the user or user group for the credential. Possible values are:
 - 32—The user can view credential information and use the credential in scans. Corresponds to the **Can Use** permission in the user interface.
 - 64—The user can view and edit credential settings, delete the credential, and use the credential in scans. Corresponds to the **Can Edit** permission in the user interface.  

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

name.description:
The name of the user or user group that you want to grant permissions for the managed credential.  

This parameter is optional when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

put.description:
Updates a managed credential object. **Note:** You cannot use this endpoint to update the credential type. If you create a managed credential with the incorrect type, create a new managed credential with the correct credential type, and delete the incorrect managed credential.
Requires Can Edit [64] credential permissions. See [Permissions](doc:permissions).

parameters.description: The UUID of the managed credential object you want to update.
parameters.name: uuid

name.description: The new name of the managed credential object. This name must be unique within your Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

description.description: The new description of the managed credential object.

ad_hoc.description: A value specifying if the credential is managed (`false`) versus stored in a scan or policy configuration (`true`). You can only set this parameter from `true` to `false`. You cannot set this parameter to `true`. If you omit this parameter, the value defaults to `false`.

password.description: The user password on the target system.

permissions.description: User permissions for the managed credential.

 - 64—The user can view and edit credential settings, delete the credential, and use the credential in scans. Corresponds to the **Can Edit** permission in the user interface.  

This parameter is required when assigning CAN USE (32) or CAN EDIT (64) permissions for a managed credential.

get.description:
Lists all credential types supported for managed credentials in Tenable Vulnerability Management. For more information about using the data returned by this endpoint to create managed credentials, see [Determine Settings for a Credential Type](doc:determine-settings-for-credential-type).
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

200.description: Returns a list of supported credential types and associated settings.

id.description: The system name that uniquely identifies the category in Tenable Vulnerability Management.

category.description: The display name for the category in the user interface.

default_expand.description: A value specifying whether the list of credential types in the category appears as expanded by default in the user interface.

types.description: Supported configuration settings for an individual credential type.

id.description: The system name that uniquely identifies the credential type.

name.description: The display name for the credential type in the user interface.

max.description: The maximum number of instances of this credential type that Tenable Vulnerability Management supports for an individual scan or policy.

configuration.description: The configuration settings for a credential type. For a definition of object attributes, see [Determine Settings for a Credential Type](docs/determine-settings-for-credential-type).

items.description: The configuration settings for the credential. The parameters of this object vary depending on the credential type. For a list of possible configuration parameters, use the GET /credentials/types endpoint.

type.description:
The parameter input type. This attribute reflects how the user interface prompts for parameter input. Possible values include: 
 - password—Prompts for input via text box.
 - text—Prompts for input via text box.
 - select—Prompts for input via selectable options.
 - file—Prompts user to upload file of input data.
 - toggle—Prompts user to toggle an option on or off.
 - checkbox—Prompts user to select options via checkboxes. Checkboxes can represent enabling a single option or can allow users to select from multiple, mutually-exclusive options.

name.description: The display name for the credential configuration in the user interface.

required.description: A value specifying whether the configuration parameter is required (`true`) or optional (`false`). If this attribute is absent, the parameter is optional.

id.description: The system name for the credential parameter. Use this value as the parameter name in request messages configuring credentials.

placeholder.description:
An example of the parameter value. This value appears as example text in the user interface.  

This attribute is only present for credential parameters that require text input in the user interface.

options.description: The supported options for the credential parameter.

name.description: The display name of the option in the user interface.

id.description: The system name for the option.

inputs.description: The additional inputs that are required if the user selects this option in the user interface. If the inputs parameter is empty (`\[\]`), selecting the option does not require additional user input.

type.description:
The type of input prompt in the user interface. Possible values include:
 - password—Prompts for input via text box.
 - text—Prompts for input via text box.
 - select—Prompts for input via selectable options.
 - file—Prompts user to upload file of input data.
 - toggle—Prompts user to select one of two mutually-exclusive options in toggle format.
 - checkbox—Prompts user to select options via checkboxes. Checkboxes can represent enabling a single option or can allow users to select from multiple, mutually-exclusive options.
 - key-value— Prompts for text entry of a key-value pair via two text boxes.

name.description: The display name of the option in the user interface.

required.description: A value specifying whether the input is required (`true`) or optional (`false`).

placeholder.description:
An example of the input value. This value appears as example text in the user interface.  

This attribute is only present for credential parameters that require text input in the interface.   

In cases where the input type is `key-value`, this attribute can be an array of strings.

regex.description: A regular expression defining the valid input for the parameter in the user interface.

hint.description: Helpful information about the input required, for example, "PEM formatted certificate". Hints appear in the user interface, but can contain information that is relevant to API requests.

callback.description: Not supported as a parameter in managed credentials.

default-row-count.description: The number of text box rows that appear by default when the input type is `key-value`.

hide-values.description: A value specifying whether the user interface hides the value by default when the input type is `key-value`. If `true`, dots appear instead of characters as you type the value in the user interface.

id.description: The system name for the input. Use this value as the input name in request messages when configuring credentials.

default.description: The option that appears as selected by default in the user interface.

alt_ids.description: Not supported as a parameter in managed credentials.

preferences.description: Not supported as a parameter in managed credentials.

expand_settings.description: A value specifying whether the configuration settings appear expanded by default in the user interface.

post.description:
Uploads a file for use with a managed credential (for example, as a private key file for an SSH credential). Tenable Vulnerability Management validates this request with the required `fileType` query parameter. The supported `fileTypes` are: `pem`, `json`, `csv`, `x.509`, `p12`, `cookie`, `ssh` (only RSA and DSA OpenSSH keys/certificates are supported). To pass validation, `cookie` files must adhere to the Netscape cookies file format. Additionally, `csv` files must adhere to one of the following formats:
  • `target, port, database_name, username, cred_manager`
  • `target, port, instance_name, username, auth_type, cred_manager`
  • `target, port, service_type, service_ID, username, auth_type, cred_manager`
  • `target, port, service_type, service_ID, username, auth_type, cred_manager, accountname_or_secretname`
For more information about using this file, see [Create a Managed Credential](doc:create-managed-credential).
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.name: fileType
parameters.description: The type of the file being uploaded.

200.description: Returns the name of the successfully uploaded file.

fileuploaded.description: The name of the uploaded file. If the file with the same name already exists, Tenable Vulnerability Management appends an underscore with a number, for example ssh_private_key_1.txt. Use this attribute value when referencing the file for subsequent requests.

400.description: Returned if your request is malformed (for example, the request is missing a required query parameter or an invalid file is provided).

value.error: The provided file is not a valid .json file.
The required query parameter fileType cannot be null or empty.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

response.value:
429 Too Many Requests

nginx

500.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management cannot upload the file.

value.message: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

post.description:
Creates a new exclusion.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

name.description: The name of the exclusion.

members.description:
The targets that you want excluded from scans. Specify multiple targets as a comma-separated string. Targets can be in the following formats:
 - An individual IPv4 address (`192.0.2.1`)
 - A range of IPv4 addresses (`192.0.2.1-192.0.2.255`)
 - CIDR notation (`192.0.2.0/24`)
 - A fully-qualified domain name (FQDN) (`host.domain.com`)

schedule.description: The schedule parameters for the exclusion.

enabled.description: If `true`, the exclusion schedule is active.

starttime.description: The start time for the exclusion formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` (ISO 8601 format).

endtime.description: The end time for the exclusion formatted as `YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` (ISO 8601 format).

rrules.description: The recurrence rules for the exclusion.

interval.description: The interval of the rule. For example, every 4 days, every 2 months, every 3 years, etc.

byweekday.description: A comma-separated string of days to repeat a WEEKLY freq rule on (SU,MO,TU,WE,TH,FR, or SA).

bymonthday.description: The day of the month to repeat a MONTHLY freq rule on.

timezone.description: The timezone for the exclusion. You can retrieve a list of timezones from the [Get timezones](ref:scans-timezones) endpoint.

network_id.description: The ID of the network object associated with scanners where Tenable Vulnerability Management applies the exclusion. The default network ID is `00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000`. To determine the ID of a custom network, use the [GET /networks](ref:networks-list) endpoint. For more information about network objects, see [Manage Networks](doc:manage-networks-tio).

200.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully created the exclusion.

 - CIDR notation (`192.0.2.0/24`)

 - CIDR notation (`192.0.2.0/24`)
 - A fully-qualified domain name (FQDN) (`host.domain.com`)

get.description:
Lists network objects for your organization.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description:
A filter condition in the following format: `field:operator:value`. For network objects, you can only filter on the `name` field, using the following operators: 
* eq—The name of the returned network object is equal to the text you specify. 
* neq—The returned list of network objects excludes the network object where the name is equal to the text you specify. 
* match—The returned list includes network objects where the name contains the text you specify at least partially.

You can specify multiple `f` parameters, separated by ampersand (&) characters. If you specify multiple `f` parameters, use the `ft` parameter to specify how Tenable Vulnerability Management applies the multiple filter conditions.
parameters.name: f

parameters.description: The operator that Tenable Vulnerability Management applies if multiple \`f\` parameters are present. The `OR` operator is the only supported value. If you omit this parameter and multiple `f` parameters are present, Tenable Vulnerability Management applies the `OR` operator by default.
parameters.name: ft

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `50`.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description: The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `name:asc` would sort results by the `name` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

parameters.description: Indicates whether Tenable Vulnerability Management includes deleted network objects in the response message. Deleted network objects contain the additional attributes, `deleted` and `deleted_by`, which specifies the date (in Unix time) when the network object was deleted and the UUID of the user that deleted the network object.

Before you delete a network object, consider moving assets to a different network using the bulk asset move endpoint ([POST /api/v2/assets/bulk-jobs/move-to-network](ref:assets-bulk-move)). If you delete a network object, Tenable Vulnerability Management: - Returns the scanners and scanner groups associated with the deleted object to the default network object. - Retains any asset records for the deleted network until the assets age out of your licensed assets count. **Note:** You can view deleted network objects using the `includeDeleted` filter in a [GET /networks](ref:networks-list) request.

409.description:
Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management determines that assigning the scanner or scanner group causes a network conflict. A response message with this code includes a response body under the following conditions:  
* The scanner you attempted to assign is a member of more than one scanner group.  
* The scanner group you attempted to assign contains one or more scanners that belong to another scanner group.

post.description:
Bulk assigns scanners and scanner groups to a custom network. The data in this request payload overwrites the full list of specified scanners and scanner groups previously assigned to the specified network object. If the payload excludes scanners or scanner groups previously assigned to the specified network object, Tenable Vulnerability Management returns those scanners and scanner groups to the default network. You cannot use this endpoint to bulk return scanners and scanner groups from multiple custom networks to the default network.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the network object where you want to bulk assign scanners and scanner groups.
parameters.name: network_id

scanner_uuids.description: A list of UUIDs for the scanners and scanner groups you want to bulk move to a network object. To get values for this list, use the [GET /networks/{network_id}/assignable-scanners](ref:networks-list-assignable-scanners) endpoint.

200.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully assigned the scanners and scanner groups to the network object.

400.description: Returned if you attempt to assign an AWS scanner to the network object. For AWS assets, use network segmentation processes offered by AWS instead.

401.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management cannot authenticate the user account that submitted the request.

403.description: Returned if you do not have sufficient permissions to assign a scanner or scanner group to the specified network object.

404.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management could not find either the network object, the scanner object, or the scanner group object you specified.

409.description:
Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management determines that assigning the scanner or scanner group causes a network conflict. A response message with this code includes a response body under the following conditions:  
* A scanner you attempted to assign is a member of more than one scanner group.  
* A scanner group you attempted to assign contains one or more scanners that belong to another scanner group.

scanner_uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner involved in the conflict.

scanner_groups.description: A list of scanner groups involved in the conflict.

uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner group.

name.description: The name of the scanner group.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

response.value:
429 Too Many Requests

nginx

value.message: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

503.description: Returned if a Tenable Vulnerability Management service is unavailable. Wait a moment, and try your request again.

value.message: Please wait a moment and try your request again.

get.description:
Lists all scanners and scanner groups not yet assigned to a custom network object. This list includes all scanner groups within the default network AND any scanners within the default network that do not belong to a scanner group.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the default network object.
parameters.name: network_id

200.description: Returns a list of assignable scanners and scanner groups in the default network object.

schema.description: A list of scanners and scanner groups.

creation_date.description: The date on which the scanner was linked to the Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

engine_build.description: The build of the engine running on the scanner.

engine_version.description: The version of the engine running on the scanner.

group.description: Indicates whether the object represents a single scanner (`false`) or a scanner group (`true`).

id.description: The unique ID of the scanner.

key.description: An alpha-numeric sequence of characters used when linking a scanner to Tenable Vulnerability Management.

last_connect.description: The Unix timestamp when the scanner last connected to the Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

last_modification_date.description: The Unix timestamp when the scanner was last updated.

linked.description: Indicates if the scanner is enabled (`1`) or not disabled (`0`).

loaded_plugin_set.description: The current plugin set on the scanner.

name.description: The user-defined name of the scanner.

num_scans.description: The number of scan tasks the scanner is currently executing.

owner.description: The scanner owner.

owner_id.description: The ID of the scanner owner.

owner_name.description: The name of the scanner owner.

owner_uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner owner.

pool.description: Indicates whether the scanner is part of a scanner group.

report_frequency.description: The frequency (in seconds) at which the scanner polls the Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

scan_count.description: The current number of scans currently running on the scanner.

status.description:
The scanner's current status. Possible values are:
 - on—The scanner has connected in the last five minutes.
 - off—The scanner has not connected in the last five minutes.

type.description: The type of scanner (local or remote).

uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner.

remote_uuid.description: The UUID of the Nessus installation on the scanner.

supports_remote_logs.description: Indicates whether the scanner supports remote logging.

post.description:
Creates an OT connector. 

The response includes a token when you create the OT connector. The token is the only credential that is used for authentication when the OT connector connects to Tenable Vulnerability Management.

**Note:** You can generate the required linking key and retrieve the target site with the [Create linking key](ref:sensors-ot-linking-key-generate) endpoint.

parameters.description: The linking key for the OT connector. You can generate the required linking key with the [Create linking key](ref:sensors-ot-linking-key-create) endpoint.
parameters.name: x-linking-key

parameters.description: The target site for the OT connector. The target site value is returned when you generate a linking key with the [Create linking key](ref:sensors-ot-linking-key-create) endpoint.
parameters.name: x-target-site

name.description: The name of the OT connector. The maximum character length is 64 chars.

subType.description: The type of OT connector. There are two types of OT connectors, Industrial Core Platform (ICP) and Enterprise Manager (EM).

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not the OT connector is enabled.

200.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully created the OT connector.

containerUuid.description: The unique ID of your Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

id.description: The unique ID of the OT connector.

name.description: The OT connectors name.

type.description: The sensor type. This value is always `ot` for OT connectors.

subType.description: The OT connector's type. There are two types of OT connectors, Industrial Core Platform (ICP) and Enterprise Manager (EM).

created.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was created.

modified.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was last modified.

lastAuth.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was last authenticated.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not the OT connector is enabled.

token.description: The credential used for authentication when the OT connector connects to Tenable Vulnerability Management.

400.description: Returned if the provided linking key does not match existing records or is expired.

value.error: Key does not exist or expired.

500.description: Returned if Tenable Vulnerability Management encountered an error while attempting to create the OT connector.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code.

message.description: A brief message describing the error Tenable Vulnerability Management encountered.
message.example: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

get.description:
Returns a list of Tenable OT connectors.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The type of connector or sensor. This value is always `ot` for OT connectors.
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: The attribute used to sort the list of OT connectors.
parameters.name: sortBy

parameters.description: The criteria used to order the list of OT connectors. If this value is omitted, Tenable uses the default value `asc`.
parameters.name: orderBy

parameters.description: The maximum number of OT connectors to return. The valid range is [1-200].
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The ID of the last OT connector included in the current result set. This parameter is used for paging through the results and it must be used in conjunction with the `exclusiveStartSortKey` parameter.
parameters.name: exclusiveStartKey

parameters.description: The value of the `sortBy` key for which to sort the returned list of OT connectors. It must be used in conjunction with the `exclusiveStartKey` parameter.
parameters.name: exclusiveStartSortKey

200.description: Returned if the Tenable Vulnerability Management successfully retrieved the list of OT connectors.

exclusiveStartKey.description: The ID of the last OT connector included in the current result set. This parameter is used for paging through the results, and it must be used in conjunction with the `exclusiveStartSortKey` parameter.

exclusiveStartSortKey.description: The value of the sortBy key for which to sort the returned list of OT connectors by. It must be used in conjunction with the `exclusiveStartKey` parameter.

containerUuid.description: The unique ID of your Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

id.description: The unique ID of the OT connector.

name.description: The OT connectors name.

type.description: The sensor type. This value is always `ot` for OT connectors.

subType.description: The OT connector's type. There are two types of OT connectors, Industrial Core Platform (ICP) and Enterprise Manager (EM).

created.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was created.

modified.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was last modified.

lastAuth.description: The Unix timestamp of when the OT connector was last authenticated.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not the OT connector is enabled.

400.description: Returned when only one of `exclusiveStartKey` or `exclusiveStartSortKey` query parameters are provided. If one of these parameters is used, both parameters must be provided together.

value.error: Both exclusiveStartKey and exclusiveStartSortKey are required.

post.description:
Generates a single-use linking key that is valid for two hours. Additionally, the target site for your account is retrieved (ex. `us-2b`). The linking key and target site are required when creating an OT connector with the [Create OT connector](ref:sensors-ot-create) endpoint. Once linked, an OT connector can transmit assets and findings data from Tenable OT Security to Tenable Vulnerability Management.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The type of connector or sensor. This value is always `ot` for OT connectors.

x-target-site.description: The target site for the OT connector. The target site value is returned when you generate a linking key with the [Create linking key](ref:sensors-ot-linking-key-create) endpoint.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code.

message.description: A brief message describing the error Tenable Vulnerability Management encountered.
message.example: An internal server error occurred. Please wait a moment and try your request again.

post.description:
Creates a new profile. The type of profile created depends on the `sensor_type` path parameter, which can be set to either `agent` or `scanners`. **Note:** You can assign or remove an agent from an agent profile using the [Assign or remove agent profile](ref:agent-bulk-operations-profile) endpoint. For scanners, use the [Assign to scanner profile](ref:scanner-profile-assign-bulk) and [Remove from scanner profile](ref:scanner-profile-remove-bulk) endpoints.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The type of sensor. Use `agent` for agent profiles or `scanners` for scanner profiles.
parameters.name: sensor_type

name.description: A name for the profile.

description.description: A short description explaining the purpose or targets of the profile.
description.example: This profile targets sensors for US-1A.

oneOf.title: Agent Profile Config
oneOf.description: Configuration settings and options to apply to the profile.

version.description: The version to apply to the members of the agent profile.

pluginset.description:
The plugin update plan configuration. You can specify what plugins to install on linked agents during the daily plugin update. You can choose from the following options:
  • Auto update to the latest—Specify the value `latest` to auto update to the latest.
  • Delay plugin updates by days—Specify a two digit number `1` to `30` indicating the number of days to delay plugin updates.
  • Select plugin set from last 30 days—Specify a plugin set from the last 30 days, for example `202407172302`. Plugin sets must be 12 digits.

immediate_plugin_update.description: Determines whether agents assigned to this profile accelerate plugin set updates. When set to `true`, agents immediately update their plugin sets after receiving a new plugin set assignment that replaces an earlier set assigned by the profile. When set to `false`, agents update their plugin sets during their regular check-in cycle, which occurs at least every 24 hours.

update_disabled.description: Indicates whether or not to exclude agents from software updates. If set to true, all agents assigned to this profile are prevented from receiving software updates. This setting overrides scheduled freeze windows.

modules.description: Configuration settings and options for the members of the profile. Nessus agent identifier service options are defined in the `com.tenable.agent_identifier_service` object, and Nessus agent settings are defined in the `com.tenable.nessus_agent` object.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable additional agent settings. Currently, the only supported setting is `backend_log_level`.

settings.description: An array of objects where each object is one individual setting. Each object contains a `setting` property to specify the setting and a `value` property to specify the value for setting. For example, `{"setting":"backend_log_level","value":"debug"}`.

setting.description: The setting to apply to the agents in the profile.

value.description: The value for the setting.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable an open agent port for the agent identifier service. This setting enables Tenable network scanners to positively identity scan targets hosting Tenable agents. These hosts will appear as a single asset regardless of whether they are the target of a network scan or are generating agent scans. Note that enabling this option configures your network scanners to probe each target system on the port you select.

open_agent_port.description: The port to use for the agent identifier service.

com.tenable.fredi_module.description:
Nessus agent continuous assessment scanning settings.

Continuous assessment scanning provides continuous monitoring and reporting of vulnerability status changes on your hosts. In the case of vulnerabilities that can be mitigated with software updates, continuous assessment scanning provides automatic remediation scan in near-time.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable continuous assessment scanning.

base_inventory_freq.description: Indicates how often the agents perform a full software inventory scan via continuous assessment scanning in days. You can choose any integer between `1` and `14`.

cpu_limit.description: Specifies the maximum percentage of the host's CPU that Windows and Linux agents can use. Supported values range from 1 to 100. **Note:**: This setting applies only to agents running version 11.1.0 or later and is not supported on macOS hosts.

oneOf.title: Scanner Profile Config
oneOf.description: Configuration settings and options to apply to the profile.

version.description: The version to apply to the members of the scanner profile.

pluginset.description:
The plugin update plan configuration. You can specify what plugins to install on linked scanners during the daily plugin update. You can choose from the following options:
  • Auto update to the latest—Specify the value `latest` to auto update to the latest.
  • Delay plugin updates by days—Specify a two digit number `1` to `30` indicating the number of days to delay plugin updates.
  • Select plugin set from last 30 days—Specify a plugin set from the last 30 days, for example `202407172302`. Plugin sets must be 12 digits.

update_disabled.description: Indicates whether or not to exclude scanners from software updates. If set to true, all scanners assigned to this profile are prevented from receiving software updates. This setting overrides scheduled freeze windows.

200.description: Returned if the profile was created successfully.

uuid.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the new profile.

400.description: Returned if your request specified invalid parameters or if your request was improperly formatted.

error.description: A brief description of the error.

401.description: Returned if the API keys specified in your request are invalid.

statusCode.description: The HTTP status code of the error.

message.description: A brief message about the cause of the error.

value.message: Invalid credentials.

The plugin update plan configuration. You can specify what plugins to install on linked agents during the daily plugin update. You can choose from the following options:
  • Auto update to the latest—Specify the value `latest` to auto update to the latest.
  • Delay plugin updates by days—Specify a two digit number `1` to `30` indicating the number of days to delay plugin updates.
  • Select plugin set from last 30 days—Specify a plugin set from the last 30 days, for example `202407172302`. Plugin sets must be 12 digits.

Note that enabling this option configures your network scanners to probe each target system on the port you select.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable continuous assessment scanning.

A value of `true` means agents update immediately, while `false` means they update during their regular check-in cycle (at least every 24 hours).

update_disabled.description: Indicates whether or not the agents are excluded from software updates. If set to true, all agents assigned to this profile are prevented from receiving software updates. This setting overrides scheduled freeze windows.

container_public_key.description: The public key for the agent identifier.

modules.description: Configuration settings and options for the members of the agent profile. Nessus agent identifier service options are defined in the `com.tenable.agent_identifier_service` object, and Nessus agent settings are defined in the `com.tenable.nessus_agent` object.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not additional agent settings are enabled. Currently, the only supported setting is `backend_log_level`.

settings.description: An array of objects where each object is one individual setting. Each object contains a `setting` property to specify the setting and a `value` property to specify the value for setting. For example, `{"setting":"backend_log_level","value":"debug"}`.

setting.description: The setting applied to the agents in the profile.

value.description: The value for the setting.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not an open agent port is enabled for the agent identifier service. This setting enables Tenable network scanners to positively identity scan targets hosting Tenable agents. These hosts will appear as a single asset regardless of whether they are the target of a network scan or are generating agent scans. Note that enabling this option configures your network scanners to probe each target system on the port you select.

open_agent_port.description: The port to used for the agent identifier service.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable continuous assessment scanning.

base_inventory_freq.description: Indicates how often the agents perform a full software inventory scan via continuous assessment scanning in days. You can choose any integer between `1` and `14`.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not the Tenable universal agent module is enabled.

resources.description: Resource usage configuration currently applied to the agent.

cpu_limit.description: The maximum percentage of the host's CPU that the agent is allowed to use. Supported values range from 1 to 100. **Note:**: This setting applies only to agents running version 11.1.0 or later and is not supported on macOS hosts.

com.tenable.fredi_module.description:
Nessus agent continuous assessment scanning settings.

Continuous assessment scanning provides continuous monitoring and reporting of vulnerability status changes on your hosts. In the case of vulnerabilities that can be mitigated with software updates, continuous assessment scanning provides automatic remediation scan in near-time.

enabled.description: Indicates whether or not to enable continuous assessment scanning.

base_inventory_freq.description: Indicates how often the agents perform a full software inventory scan via continuous assessment scanning in days. You can choose any integer between `1` and `14`.

cpu_limit.description: Specifies the maximum percentage of the host's CPU that Windows and Linux agents can use. Supported values range from 1 to 100. **Note:**: This setting applies only to agents running version 11.1.0 or later and is not supported on macOS hosts.

delete.description:
Deletes the specified profile. The type of profile to delete depends on the `sensor_type` path parameter, which can be set to either `agent` or `scanners`. You can monitor the status of deletion request via the [Get agent task status](ref:bulk-task-agent-status) or [Get scanner task status](ref:scanner-task-status) endpoint. **Note:** If the agent or scanner profile has any assigned agents or scanners, they will be reassigned to the default agent or scanner profile.

  • `FAILED`—There was an error completing the task.
  • `STALE`—The task has not been updated in a long time.

post.description:
Creates a new recast or accept rule.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role or one of the following custom role privileges: `TIO_BACKEND.RECAST_RULE.MANAGE` for Vulnerability Management or `WAS.RECAST_RULE.MANAGE` for Web App Scanning. See [Roles](doc:roles).

rule_name.description:
A name for the rule. 

If not provided, a default is generated using the following format: rule type (`Recast` or `Accept`), the plugin ID (for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications) or the audit file name (for Host Audits), and the rule creation date. For example, `Recast - Plugin ID: 123642 - Created: 2023-03-14`.

resource_type.description: Specifies the type of findings that the rule will target.

rule_value.description: Defines the configuration of the rule based on the selected action.

action.description:
Specifies the type of rule to apply. Values are case sensitive.

 Options include `RECAST` or `ACCEPT` for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications, and `CHANGE_RESULT` or `ACCEPT_RESULT` for Host Audits.

original_severity.description: The original severity of the plugin. Values are case sensitive.

severity.description:
For `RECAST` rules only. Specifies the new severity level to assign to the targeted vulnerabilities.

 **Note:** This parameter is required for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications. Values are case sensitive.

compliance_result.description:
For `CHANGE_RESULT` rules only. Specifies the new result to assign to the targeted Host Audits.

**Note:** This parameter is required for Host Audits. Values are case sensitive.

comment.description: Optional notes or rationale for the rule.

false_positive.description: Indicates that the finding is a false positive and should be reviewed by Tenable.

filter.description: Filter rules based on the targeted vulnerabilities. Use the `and` array to combine multiple conditions that all must be true. Use the `or` array to combine multiple conditions where at least one must be true.

put.description:
Updates the specified recast or accept rule with the specified criteria.
Requires the Administrator [64] user role or one of the following custom role privileges: `TIO_BACKEND.RECAST_RULE.MANAGE` for Vulnerability Management or `WAS.RECAST_RULE.MANAGE` for Web App Scanning. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the recast rule.
parameters.name: rule_id

rule_name.description:
A name for the rule. 

If not provided, a default is generated using the following format: rule type (`Recast` or `Accept`), the plugin ID (for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications) or the audit file name (for Host Audits), and the rule creation date. For example, `Recast - Plugin ID: 123642 - Created: 2023-03-14`.

resource_type.description: Specifies the type of findings that the rule will target.

rule_value.description: Defines the configuration of the rule based on the selected action.

action.description:
Specifies the type of rule to apply. Values are case sensitive.

 Options include `RECAST` or `ACCEPT` for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications, and `CHANGE_RESULT` or `ACCEPT_RESULT` for Host Audits.

original_severity.description: The original severity of the plugin. Values are case sensitive.

severity.description:
For `RECAST` rules only. Specifies the new severity level to assign to the targeted vulnerabilities.

 **Note:** This parameter is required for Vulnerabilities and Web Applications. Values are case sensitive.

compliance_result.description:
For `CHANGE_RESULT` rules only. Specifies the new result to assign to the targeted Host Audits.

**Note:** This parameter is required for Host Audits. Values are case sensitive.

comment.description: Optional notes or rationale for the rule.

false_positive.description: Indicates that the finding is a false positive and should be reviewed by Tenable.

get.description:
Returns the scanner list.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

200.description: Returned if the list of scanners was retrieved successfully.

creation_date.description: The Unix timestamp when the scanner was created. This attribute specifies the original creation date if the scanner was migrated.

group.description: Indicates whether the scanner belongs to a scanner group ('true') or not ('false').

groups.description: A list of scanner groups that the scanner belongs to.

name.description: The name of the scanner group.

uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner group.

id.description: The unique ID of the scanner.

ip_addresses.description: A list of IP addresses associated with the scanner.

key.description: The linking key, that is, the alpha-numeric sequence of characters you use to link a scanner to Tenable Vulnerability Management. For more information about linking a scanner, see the [Link a Sensor](https://docs.tenable.com/vulnerability-management/Content/Settings/Sensors/LinkaSensor.htm) in the Tenable Vulnerability Management User Guide.

last_connect.description: The Unix timestamp when any of the scanner's tasks have provided its last update.

last_modification_date.description: The Unix timestamp when the scanner was last modified.

linked.description: Specifies whether you disabled (`0`) or enabled (`1`) the scanner. For more information, see the [PUT /scanners/{scanner_id}/link](ref:scanners-toggle-link-state) endpoint.

loaded_plugin_set.description: The current plugin set on the scanner.

registration_code.description: The registration code of the scanner.

type.description: The license type.

ips.description: The number of hosts the scanner is licensed to use.

agents.description: The number of agents the scanner is licensed to use.

scanners.description: The number of scanners the scanner is licensed to use.

type.description: The Tenable products licensed on the scanner.
Indicates that the scanner is licensed to perform Tenable Container Security scans.

mode.description: Indicates whether the product license is an evaluation license (`eval`) or standard license (`standard`).

expiration_date.description: The Unix timestamp when the license expires.

activation_code.description: The activation code you used to enable the license. This value is present for standard licenses only.

max_gb.description: The maximum memory (in GB) on the scanner allotted for the Tenable licensed application.

type.description: Indicates that the scanner is licensed to perform Tenable Web App Scanning scans.

mode.description: Indicates whether the product license is an evaluation license (`eval`) or standard license (`standard`).

expiration_date.description: The Unix timestamp when the license expires.

activation_code.description: The activation code you used to enable the license. This value is present for standard licenses only.

web_assets.description: The number of web assets which your license authorizes you to scan.

name.description: The user-defined name of the scanner.

network_name.description: The name of the network object associated with the scanner. For more information about network objects, see [Manage Networks](doc:manage-networks-tio).

num_scans.description: The number of scans (tasks) the scanner is currently executing.

owner.description: The owner of the scanner.

owner_id.description: The ID of the owner of the scanner.

owner_name.description: The username of the owner of the scanner.

owner_uuid.description: The UUID of the owner of the scanner.

pool.description: Indicates whether the scanner is part of a scanner group ('true') or not ('false'). For more information about scanner groups, see the [Scanner Groups](ref:scanner-groups) endpoints.

scan_count.description: The number of scans that the scanner is currently running.

shared.description: Indicates whether anyone other than the scanner owner has explicit access to the scanner (`1`).

status.description: The status of the scanner (`on` or `off`).

type.description: The type of scanner (`local`, `managed`, `managed_pvs`, `pool`, `remote`, or `webapp`).

ui_build.description: The backend build of Nessus that is running on the scanner.

ui_version.description: The backend version of Nessus that is running on the scanner.

user_permissions.description: The permissions you (the current user) have been assigned for the scanner. See [Permissions](doc:permissions).

uuid.description: The UUID of the scanner.

remote_uuid.description: The UUID of the Nessus installation on the scanner.

supports_remote_logs.description: Indicates if the scanner supports remote logging.

supports_webapp.description: Indicates if the scanner supports Tenable Web App Scanning.

aws_update_interval.description: Specifies how often, in minutes, the scanner checks in with Tenable Vulnerability Management (Amazon Web Services scanners only).

profile_uuid.description: The unique ID (UUID) of the scanner profile that the scanner belongs to.

profile_name.description: The name of the scanner profile that the scanner belongs to.

403.description: Returned if you do not have permission to view the list.

429.description: Returned if you attempt to send too many requests in a specific period of time. For more information, see [Rate Limiting](doc:rate-limiting).

Use the `request_status_id` or `id` value returned by supported endpoints to track the progress or completion of the asynchronous task.

200.description: Returned if the status of the asynchronous scanner task was retrieved successfully.

container_uuid.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the container where the task took place.

type.description: The type of asynchronous task. For scanners, the type is always `service-scanner-bulk-operation`.

id.description: The unique identifier (UUID) for tracking the status of the asynchronous task. This identifier corresponds to the `request_status_id` or `id` returned by supported endpoints.

status.description: The status of the asynchronous operation. The possible statuses are `completed`, `received`, `processing`, and `aborted`.

created.description: A Unix timestamp indicating the date and time when the asynchronous task was created.

modified.description: A Unix timestamp indicating the date and time when the asynchronous task was modified.

post.description:
Create instructions for a single scanner to perform.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description: The UUID of the scanner.
parameters.name: scanner_uuid

type.description: The type of instruction to perform. To restart the scanner service, choose `restart`. To modify settings, choose `settings`.

options.description: Specifies the instructions you wish to send to the scanner. The `options` object is different depending on whether you want to restart the scanner service or change settings for the scanner.

oneOf.title: Restart Scanner Service (OPTION 1)
oneOf.description: The options for the restart scanner service operation.

hard.description: Indicates whether or not to perform a hard restart of the scanner service. A hard restart will inform the scanner service to restart immediately regardless of what it is doing.

idle.description: Indicates whether or not to restart the scanner service when the scanning engine is idle.

oneOf.title: Change Settings (OPTION 2)
oneOf.description: The settings to change.

settings.description: An array of objects where each object is one individual setting or instruction to perform. Each object contains a `setting` property to specify the setting and a `value` property to specify the value for setting. For example, `{"setting":"backend_log_level","value":"debug"}`.

setting.description: The setting to send to the scanner. Supported settings are `backend_log_level`, `auto_update`, and `auto_update_delay`. For information about these settings and a list of possible values to use with the settings, see [Advanced Settings](https://docs.tenable.com/nessus/Content/SettingsAdvanced.htm) in the Nessus User Guide.

value.description: The value to use for the specified setting. Choose either a single string value or a single integer value to use for the value.

type.description: The type of instruction, either `restart` or `settings`.

status.description: The status of the scan task. For a list of scan statuses and their descriptions, see [Scan Status](doc:scan-status-tio).

created.description: The creation date of the directive in unix time milliseconds.

modified.description: The date the directive was last modified in unix time milliseconds.

results_blob_size.description: The size of the log that the scanner returns when performing a log directive. This value is always `0` for non-log directives.

value.description: The value of the setting.

ttl.description: The time to live for the directive. The status is updated to `failed` if the directive is not executed within the TTL time frame.

id.description: The UUID of the directive task.

post.description:
Creates a new scanner group. You cannot use this endpoint to:
  • Assign the new scanner group to a network object. Tenable Vulnerability Management automatically assigns new scanner groups to the default network object. To assign a scanner group to a network object, use the [POST /networks/{network_id}/scanners/{scanner_uuid}](ref:networks-assign-scanner) endpoint.
  • Configure scan routes for the scanner group. Instead, use the [PUT /scanner-groups/group_id/routes](ref:io-scanner-groups-update-routes) endpoint.

get.description:
Returns a list of tag categories.
Requires the Basic [16] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

parameters.description:
A filter condition in the `field:operator:value` format, for example, `f=name:match:location`. Filter conditions can include:  
* name:eq:<name>  
* name:match:<partial\_name>  
* description:eq:<description>  
* description:match:<partial\_description>  
* updated\_at:date-eq:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* updated\_at:date-gt:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* updated\_at:date-lt:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* created\_at:date-eq:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* created\_at:date-gt:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* created\_at:date-lt:<timestamp\_as\_int>  
* updated\_by:eq:<user\_uuid>
parameters.name: f

parameters.description: If multiple `f` parameters are present, specifies whether Tenable Vulnerability Management applies `AND` or `OR` to conditions. Supported values are `and` and `or`. If you omit this parameter when using multiple `f` parameters, Tenable Vulnerability Management applies `AND` by default.
parameters.name: ft

parameters.description: The number of records to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `5000`.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: The starting record to retrieve. If this parameter is omitted, Tenable Vulnerability Management uses the default value of `0`.
parameters.name: offset

parameters.description:
The field you want to use to sort the results by along with the sort order. The field is specified first, followed by a colon, and the order is specified second (`asc` or `desc`). For example, `name:desc` would sort results by the `name` field in descending order.

If you specify multiple fields, the fields must be separated by commas. For example, `name:desc,updated_at:asc` would first sort results by the `name` field in descending order and then by the `updated_at` field in ascending order.
parameters.name: sort

200.description: Returned if the list of tag categories was retrieved successfully.

uuid.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of the category.

name.description: The name of the category. The name must be unique within a Tenable Vulnerability Management instance.

created_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the category was created, for example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

created_by.description: The name of the user who created the category.

updated_at.description: An ISO timestamp indicating the date and time on which the category was last updated, for example, `2018-12-31T13:51:17.243Z`.

updated_by.description: The name of the user who last updated the category.

reserved.description: Indicates whether the tags in this category are reserved (cannot be updated). This is a read-only field set by the system.

pagination.description: An object containing pagination information for the current result set.

total.description: The total number of records matching your search criteria.

limit.description: The number of records requested (or the default value if omitted from the request).

offset.description: The starting record you requested (or zero if omitted).

sort.description: An array of objects specifying how the current result set is sorted.

items.description: An object specifying how the current result set is sorted, including the property by which the results were sorted and the sort direction.

name.description: The property used to sort the results.

order.description: The sort direction for the results. `asc` for ascending or `desc` for descending.

400.description:
Returned if your request specifies invalid or malformed query parameters. Tenable Vulnerability Management can encounter the following error conditions:
 - `invalidvalue`—The query parameter format is incorrect, for example, uses an invalid operator as in this example: `f=name:invalid_operator:some_value`.
- `unknownproperty`—The query parameter format is correct, but it references a field that does not exist, for example `sort=non_existing_property:desc`.

post.description:
Creates a tag value. The tag category can be specified by UUID or name. If Tenable Vulnerability Management cannot find a category you specify by name, the system creates a new category with the specified name. To automatically apply the tag to assets, specify the rules using the `filters` property.
Requires the Scan Manager [40] user role. See [Roles](doc:roles).

category_name.description: The name of the tag category to associate with the new value. Specify the name of a new category if you want to add both a new category and tag value. Specify the name of an existing category if you want to add the tag value to the existing category. The `category_name` can result in the following responses: - If the `category_name` you specify exists, and the tag value you specify already exists for that category, Tenable Vulnerability Management returns a 400 response code instead of adding the tag. - If the `category_name` you specify exists, but the tag value you specify does not yet exist for that category, Tenable Vulnerability Management adds the tag value to the existing category. - If the `category_name` you specify does not exist, Tenable Vulnerability Management creates a new tag category and adds the new tag value to that category. This parameter is required if `category_uuid` is not present in the request message.

category_uuid.description:
The unique identifier (UUID) of the tag category to associate with the new value. For more information on determining this value, see [Determine Tag Identifiers](doc:determine-tag-identifiers-tio).

Use this parameter only if you want to add the tag value to an existing category. If the UUID you specify does not exist, Tenable Vulnerability Management does not create a new catgory. Instead, it returns a 400 (Bad Request) response code.

This parameter is required if category_name is not present in the request message.

category_description.description: The description for the new tag category that Tenable Vulnerability Management creates if the category specified by name does not exist. Otherwise, Tenable Vulnerability Management ignores the description.

value.description: The new tag value. Note that tag values cannot exceed 50 characters in length and must not contain commas.

access_control.description:
Determines what the user can do with the tag value.

**Caution:** Most customers have been migrated to role-based access control and should use the [POST /api/v3/access-control/permissions](ref:io-v3-access-control-permission-create) endpoint to assign permissions to tag objects. The `access_control` body parameter object only works for customers whose containers have not yet been migrated to role-based access control.

current_user_permissions.description: List of all permissions for the current user on the current tag. Possible values are `ALL`, `CAN_EDIT`, and `CAN_SET_PERMISSIONS`.

defined_domain_permissions.description: List of all defined permissions for objects of type tag. Possible values are `ALL`, `CAN_EDIT`, and `CAN_SET_PERMISSIONS`. Note that in responses, this field is only returned for administrators and tag owners.

all_users_permissions.description: List of the minimum set of permissions all users have on the current tag. Possible values are `ALL`, `CAN_EDIT`, and `CAN_SET_PERMISSIONS`. Note that in responses, this field is only returned for administrators and tag owners.

current_domain_permissions.description: List of user and group-specific permissions for the current tag.

id.description: The unique identifier (UUID) of a user or group.

name.description: The name of the user or group.

type.description: The type of permission.

permissions.description: The permissions associated with the user or group.

version.description: Optional, user-defined value. This value must be incremented each time the permissions are updated. Set to `1` by default.

filters.description: The filters (conditional sets of rules) for automatically applying the tag to assets. For more information, see [Apply Dynamic Tags](doc:apply-dynamic-tags).

asset.description:
The object containing conditional sets of rules for applying the tags. 

**Note:** Tenable Vulnerability Management supports a maximum of 1,000 rules per tag. This limit means that you can specify a maximum of 1,000 `and` or `or` conditions for a single tag value. Additionally, Tenable Vulnerability Management supports a maximum of 1,024 values in a comma-delimited string per individual tag rule. Note that the request body is limited to 1 MB. For more information about asset tags and their limitations, see [Manage Asset Tags](doc:manage-asset-tags-tio).

and.description:
To apply the tag to assets that match all of the rules, specify the rules inside the `and` object.

**Note:** The value you specify for the rule attribute is not case sensitive. For example, if you type the hostname "hostx," the rule matches to assets with hostname "HOSTX" and "hostx."

items.description: A rule for applying the tag to assets by matching asset properties or other tags. Includes a field or tag name, an operator, and a value.

property.description: The asset attribute or tag to match. To find supported asset attributes, use the [List asset tag filters](ref:tags-list-asset-filters) endpoint.

operator.description: The operator to apply to the matched value, for example, equals, does not equal, or contains. To find supported operators for the associated asset attribute, use the [List asset tag filters](ref:tags-list-asset-filters) endpoint.

value.description:
The asset attribute value or tag to match. For example, `192.0.2.57`. 

Select **single string value** when you have a single input value, for example, a single IP address. Select **multiple string values** to use an array of strings when you have multiple input values, for example, when you have multiple IP addresses.

or.description:
To apply the tag to assets that match any of the rules, specify the rules inside the `or` object.

**Note:** The value you specify for the rule attribute is not case sensitive. For example, if you type the hostname "hostx," the rule matches to assets with hostname "HOSTX" and "hostx."
